_comment: Radio definitions for the KDH bootloader flasher. Community contributions welcome — add your radio and submit a PR. Set 'tested' to true only after confirming a successful flash.
_variant_groups_comment: Hardware-variant groups. When one physical model ships as multiple non-interchangeable firmware variants behind a single vendor bundle, the sibling radio entries keep their own ids/urls/firmware_filename_pattern and each carries a 'variant_group' field naming its group. The top-level 'variant_groups' block describes each group: 'name' + 'manufacturer' (dropdown family label), 'firmware_page' (where to confirm hardware), 'question' + 'steps' (English identification prose, translated via variant_group.<group_id>.<field>), and an ordered 'options' list mapping each answer (options[].label, English; translated per-member via radio.<id>.variant_label) to exactly one member 'radio_id'. Ids are a compatibility surface — never rename or remove them.

radios.name: BTECH BF-F8HP Pro (NRF)
radios.bootloader_keys: SK1 + SK2 (top and bottom side buttons, not PTT). Volume to max. Press cable connector firmly into radio and hold pressure during entire flash.
radios.notes: Confirmed working on V0.53 firmware. The K1 connector may need firm pressure to maintain the data return contact on the 2.5mm ring. Keep steady pressure on the cable plug during the entire flash process.

radios.name: BTECH BF-F8HP Pro (NRFB)
radios.bootloader_keys: SK1 + SK2 (top and bottom side buttons, not PTT). Volume to max. Press cable connector firmly into radio and hold pressure during entire flash.
radios.notes: The K1 connector may need firm pressure to maintain the data return contact on the 2.5mm ring. Keep steady pressure on the cable plug during the entire flash process.

radios.name: Baofeng UV-25 Plus / UV-25 Pro
radios.notes: Uses same KDH bootloader. Firmware available from baofengradio.com. Do not use if your UV-25 does not have GPS — it will cause the radio to malfunction.

radios.name: Radtel RT-470
radios.notes: Uses WLT2000 CPS. KDH bootloader with .kdhx firmware. This URL is for the NEW PCB (V2.13C, AM/FM modulation 18MHz-1000MHz, 8.33kHz air band). Old PCB (10W) uses different firmware — see radtels.com.

radios.name: Radtel RT-490
radios.notes: Shares the JJCC JC-8629 PCB, KDH bootloader. This URL is the OLD-PCB bundle (v1.03). Heads up: that bundle was found to contain four .kdhx files (GPS / No-GPS x hardware V1.0 / V2.0) with no known reliable way to tell them apart, so the automatic download stops safely at the multiple-firmware-files guard without flashing anything. Community help identifying these sub-variants is wanted — confirm which firmware fits your radio at https://www.radtels.com/pages/software-download before flashing.

radios.name: Radtel RT-490 (New PCB)
radios.notes: Radtel RT-490 with the NEW PCB. Only use firmware v1.23a/v1.24a/v1.25a — do NOT use V2.11A or old-PCB firmware, it will brick the radio. Same JJCC JC-8629 platform. Download firmware from radtels.com or browse for a .kdhx file.

radios.name: Socotran JC8629
radios.bootloader_keys: Hold PTT while powering on (radio LED turns green = bootloader mode)
radios.notes: Same Math Mark JC-8629/8630 PCB as the Radtel RT-490. The Radtel RT-490 firmware (.kdhx) is known to work — download it from the Radtel page or use the RT-490 entry's auto-download, then come back here to flash. Source: CHIRP issue #9665 registers all 8 clones in one driver class.

radios.name: Socotran FB8629
radios.bootloader_keys: Hold PTT while powering on (radio LED turns green = bootloader mode)
radios.notes: Same Math Mark JC-8629/8630 PCB as the Radtel RT-490 — registered as a separate alias in CHIRP. The Radtel RT-490 .kdhx firmware works. Source: CHIRP issue #9665.

radios.name: Jianpai 8800 Plus
radios.bootloader_keys: Hold PTT while powering on (radio LED turns green = bootloader mode)
radios.notes: Same Math Mark JC-8629/8630 PCB as the Radtel RT-490. Sometimes labeled '8629' on resellers, but CHIRP registers this as 'Jianpai 8800 Plus'. Use the Radtel RT-490 .kdhx firmware. Source: CHIRP issue #9665.

radios.name: Boristone 8RS
radios.bootloader_keys: Hold PTT while powering on (radio LED turns green = bootloader mode). If unsure, fall back to the JJCC JC-8629 instructions.
radios.notes: Same Math Mark JC-8629/8630 PCB as the Radtel RT-490. CHIRP attachment 'Boristone_8RS_EN.img' is byte-size-identical to the MMLradio image, confirming shared firmware. Use the Radtel RT-490 .kdhx firmware. Source: CHIRP issue #9665.

radios.name: Abbree AR-869
radios.bootloader_keys: Hold PTT while powering on (radio LED turns green = bootloader mode)
radios.notes: Same Math Mark JC-8629/8630 PCB as the Radtel RT-490. The Abbree download index does not list the AR-869 individually — use the Radtel RT-490 .kdhx firmware. Source: CHIRP issue #9665.

radios.name: HamGeek HG-590
radios.bootloader_keys: Hold PTT while powering on (radio LED turns green = bootloader mode). If unsure, fall back to the JJCC JC-8629 instructions.
radios.notes: Same Math Mark JC-8629/8630 PCB as the Radtel RT-490. The HamGeek firmware index does not list the HG-590 individually — use the Radtel RT-490 .kdhx firmware. Source: CHIRP issue #9665.

radios.name: MMLradio 8629
radios.bootloader_keys: Hold PTT while powering on (radio LED turns green = bootloader mode)
radios.notes: Math Mark / MMLradio is the OEM that designs and manufactures the JC-8629/8630 PCB sold under all the other clone names. Use the Radtel RT-490 .kdhx firmware. Source: CHIRP issue #9665.

radios.name: Radtel RT-950 Pro
radios.bootloader_keys: Hold both side keys (Side1 + Side2) while powering on. The LCD shows "UPDATE" when bootloader is active.
radios.notes: AT32F403A MCU with .BTF firmware container. The BTF file is sent to the radio as-is — the on-radio bootloader decrypts it using the 16-byte XOR key embedded at file offset 0x400. Vendor distributes firmware via the RT-950 Pro owners' Facebook group; community mirrors at https://github.com/Hertzz58/Radtel-RT950-Pro-Firmware. UNTESTED on real hardware — community confirmation requested before relying on this for production flashing.

radios.name: Other KDH Radio
radios.bootloader_keys: Check your radio's manual for bootloader key combination
radios.notes: Generic option for any radio using the KDH bootloader. Browse for a .kdhx firmware file you have downloaded from your manufacturer. Many radios based on KeDiHeng designs (Abbree, Hamgeek, Socotran, JJCC, etc.) use this bootloader.

bf-f8hp-pro-family.name: BTECH BF-F8HP Pro
bf-f8hp-pro-family.question: Which hardware version is your BF-F8HP Pro?
bf-f8hp-pro-family.steps: With the radio OFF, hold the 8 key while turning it on. The display shows either NRF or NRFB.

rt-490-family.name: Radtel RT-490
rt-490-family.question: Which PCB revision is your RT-490?
rt-490-family.steps: Check whether your RT-490 can edit channel names in its menu. The new PCB supports channel-name editing; the old PCB does not.
